Harbor Notes

A field guide for tides, cargo manifests, crew journals, and mechanical rituals. Built for long reads, brisk edits, and archives that survive damp air and rough crossings.

Departure Ritual

  • Boilers inspected at 04:45.
  • Crates sealed with resin wax.
  • Signals tested on channels amber, blue, and red.
  • Every hatch tagged with cargo weight and destination.

Dock Ledger

Invoices were stamped in triplicate, then pinned to cedar boards by berth number. The oldest entries remain legible because the copies were written in dense iron gall ink with a flourish-heavy hand.

Night Watch

The harbor glass showed a clean horizon at midnight. Wind shifted east by one point, lanterns were trimmed, and the crane belts were cleaned of sand before first light.

Harbor Notes Archive 1976 · Edition 14 · Printed on weatherproof rag paper.
